_comment: Localhost seccomp profile for the packet-analysis sandbox (ADR-0031 §3). Deny-by-default (SCMP_ACT_ERRNO) with an allow-list covering the file-read, memory, and process-lifecycle syscalls the tshark child and the Python/pyshark parent require. EXPLICITLY ABSENT (and therefore denied by the default action): socket/AF_PACKET, bpf, ptrace, mount, unshare, setns, keyctl, add_key, request_key, process_vm_readv/writev, kexec, init_module, and personality. This profile is shipped here for the Helm chart (referenced via securityContext.seccompProfile.localhostProfile=netops/packet-analysis-seccomp.json under the kubelet seccomp root) and mirrored byte-for-byte to deploy/docker/seccomp/packet-analysis-seccomp.json for the Compose security_opt path (ADR-0031 §3 lockstep). RuntimeDefault is the floor we will not ship below; this Localhost profile is the target because the runtime default still permits socket/bpf/unshare.

syscalls.comment: file I/O and directory ops: read the pcap, write to the bounded /tmp scratch (ADR-0031 §2). No socket/network syscalls here.
memory management for the C dissectors and the Python heap. mprotect is allowed for the runtime; W^X is enforced by readOnlyRootFilesystem + no-new-privs, not by denying mprotect.

process/thread lifecycle so the Python parent can spawn the tshark child (argv, not shell — ADR-0023 §1) and reap it. clone/clone3 flags that create new namespaces are still blocked because unshare/setns are denied by the default action.

benign process introspection + time + RNG that glibc/Python need at startup. prctl is allowed for PR_SET_NAME/PR_SET_NO_NEW_PRIVS; capset is NOT allowed (the parser drops to zero caps and never re-acquires). Note the absence of socket(), bpf(), ptrace(), mount(), unshare(), setns(), keyctl(), add_key(), request_key(), process_vm_readv/writev(), kexec_load(), init_module(), finit_module(), and personality() — each is a known sandbox-escape or raw-socket primitive and is denied by SCMP_ACT_ERRNO.

runc >=1.2 container init (new mount API). fsopen/fsconfig/fsmount/fspick/open_tree/move_mount/mount_setattr are required by runc's safe re-open of /proc/self/fd — the CVE-2024-21626 ("Leaky Vessels") mitigation — and statfs/fstatfs verify the reopened handle is really procfs (PROC_SUPER_MAGIC). Without these the analysis container cannot start and crash-loops: 'reopen exec fifo: get safe /proc/thread-self/fd handle: fstatfs fsmount:fscontext:proc: operation not permitted'. These mirror moby's own default hardened profile. They are INERT without CAP_SYS_ADMIN, which this sandbox drops (cap_drop:ALL + no-new-privileges): the unprivileged parser still cannot mount a filesystem, pivot_root, or change namespaces — the classic mount()/unshare()/setns() escape primitives remain denied by the default action (SCMP_ACT_ERRNO).

celery/billiard POSIX named-semaphore creation: glibc sem_open(O_CREAT) atomically creates /dev/shm/sem.<name> via a temp-file-then-link() dance, so a denied link() crashes the celery prefork worker at boot ('PermissionError: [Errno 1] Operation not permitted' in _billiard.SemLock). link/linkat create hard links only (no new content, same filesystem, write required on the target dir — reachable only inside the writable tmpfs scratch and /dev/shm here); they are NOT a sandbox-escape primitive and are allowed unconditionally by moby's default profile. The pcap is on a read-only mount and cannot be hard-linked out.
